Data were converted into absolute neutron yields by
relating them to the absolute neutron group abundances
of U-235, which are well known (see G.R.Keepin,
Physics of Nuclear Kinetic, Add.Wesley,NY (1965)). The
neutron-decay curve of 32 unseparated samples of
irradiated 235U (0.01 mg) was analyzed in terms of the
six neutron groups characterized by periods of 55,22,6,
2,0.6 and 0.2 sec. From the initial activities of these
periods follows a calibration of the neutron counter in
contamination in the irradiated sample, as
8*10-3% bromine and iodine, and noble gases
